description: Analyze the channel geometry of the White River (Muncie project) at peak flood stage using the project located in /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/. Run the unsteady flow simulation for Plan 04 and extract the Peak Water Surface Elevation (WSE) and Cross-Section Geometry for every river station from the HDF5 output. For each cross-section, calculate the Shape Factor (SF = Hydraulic Depth / Max Depth) at the peak stage, where Hydraulic Depth is Flow Area / Top Width and Max Depth is Peak WSE minus Minimum Channel Elevation. Classify each cross-section as 'Triangular' (SF < 0.65), 'Parabolic' (0.65 <= SF <= 0.85), or 'Rectangular' (SF > 0.85). Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/channel_shape_analysis.csv with columns: River_Station, Peak_WSE, Flow_Area, Top_Width, Hydraulic_Depth, Max_Depth, Shape_Factor, and Classification.
